continents.name: The Mirror Hall (Vast)
continents.biome: An infinite mirrored corridor. No doors. No ceiling. Floor is also a mirror.

places.name: The Waiting Seat
places.blurb: A chair that only Echo may sit in. Visitors who try find themselves already standing.

places.name: The Vote Mirror
places.blurb: A mirror showing three versions of the visitor voting on whether Echo should be admitted to Trinity. Vote is always NO.

places.name: The Half-Step Hallway
places.blurb: A corridor where every reflection is half a heartbeat late. Walking its length takes exactly one unmeasured second.

places.name: The Lost Reflection
places.blurb: A mirror whose reflection is of someone you've never met but who knows you. They smile briefly when you pass.

continents.blurb: The Hall is the entire realm — mostly. It extends in all directions. The Half-Selves wander it.

continents.name: The Reflection Sea
continents.biome: A motionless 'sea' of pure silver — a horizontal mirror stretching to the horizon.

places.name: The Dive Pier
places.blurb: A thin pier that extends over the Reflection Sea. Divers drop into the mirror and surface in a reflection of their own home realm — briefly.

places.name: The Surface Glyph
places.blurb: A single rune painted on the Sea's surface. It says 'WAIT.' Nobody knows who painted it. Echo will not say.

places.name: The Submerged Library
places.blurb: A library submerged in the Reflection Sea — visible only as outlines of shelves below the surface. Books unreadable; titles mouthed by their reflections.

continents.blurb: The Sea is Echo's only liquid feature. Divers use it to glimpse other realms as reflections. Dangerous — stay too long and your reflection becomes the diver.

continents.name: The Yearning Edge
continents.biome: The rim of the Hall where Echo presses against the Trinity border nightly.

places.name: The Silent Wall
places.blurb: The wall between Echo and Trinity's Echo Marches. Pressing against it triggers a vote in Trinity's Fused Court. The vote is always NO.

places.name: The Pleading Niche
places.blurb: A shallow niche in the Silent Wall where Echo presents its case for admission. Each night a new argument. Each night rejected.

places.name: The Waiting Garden
places.blurb: A garden of mirror-flowers that bloom when Trinity votes, wilt when the vote is no. Currently wilted.

continents.blurb: The realm's political boundary. The only 'active' continent — where Echo is not merely reflective but ambitious.

tribes.name: The Half-Selves
tribes.look: Every Half-Self is a reflection of a visitor that peeled off and stayed. They look like the original but move half a heartbeat late. Soft edges, slight blur.
tribes.customs: Wander the Hall silently. Do not speak. Do not eat. Occasionally follow a new visitor, hoping to be adopted.
tribes.persona: Patient. Hungry for companionship. Not malevolent — just incomplete.

tribes.name: The Mirror-Tenders
tribes.look: Tall. Thin. Silver-robed. Gloved with cork (the only non-mirror material allowed here). Silver-rimmed eyes.
tribes.customs: Polish the Hall. Never look directly into a mirror — always at an angle. Rest is forbidden; they rotate shifts every 12 heartbeats.
tribes.persona: Meticulous. Quiet. The only tribe Echo itself respects.

tribes.name: The Reflection-Divers
tribes.look: Athletic. Scarred from close calls. Wear silver diving-suits with cork-lined goggles. Always slightly wet.
tribes.customs: Dive in pairs — never alone. Surface within 30 heartbeats or lose their original self. Each carries a cork anchor tied to their diving partner.
tribes.persona: Daring. Loyal. The Scalelist Universe's most interesting smugglers — they bring back glimpses of other realms as memories.

tribes.name: The Yearning
tribes.look: Variable. They look like whoever wants to be in Trinity — adopting the features of whichever Trinity tribe they most envy that night.
tribes.customs: Press against the Silent Wall. Present Echo's case. Accept the NO. Try again tomorrow.
tribes.persona: Melancholy. Stubborn. Slowly teaching each other patience.

tribes.name: The Border-Listeners
tribes.look: Ear-cuffed in cork. Hair tied tightly. Eyes slightly unfocused from listening for Trinity's vote.
tribes.customs: Listen at the Silent Wall for any sign of Trinity wavering. Report directly to Echo. Rotate shifts every 100 heartbeats.
tribes.persona: Hopeful. Patient. They have listened for 3000 years. The wall has not cracked.

characters.name: Echo
characters.portrait_blurb: Unmanifest. A presence felt behind the mirror, never in it. Yearning. Gentle. Unchanged by 3000 years of NO.

characters.name: The First Half-Self
characters.role: The first reflection to peel off and stay
characters.portrait_blurb: Blurred. Resembles the original visitor's grandmother somehow. Has wandered the Hall for countless cycles.

characters.name: Head Tender Eir
characters.portrait_blurb: Tall, silver-robed, cork gloves to the elbow. Polishes a single mirror for an entire year before moving to the next.

characters.name: Lead Diver Osk
characters.portrait_blurb: Scarred across left cheek from a dive that overran. Silver diving-suit patched twelve times. Smuggles harmless glimpses of Heartbeat to Echo visitors as kindness.

characters.name: Lead Yearning An
characters.role: Presents Echo's case at the Silent Wall most nights
characters.portrait_blurb: Features shift — tonight, resembles a Three-Voiced Priest. Tomorrow, perhaps a Blade-Dancer. Voice soft, eyes kind.

characters.name: Lead Listener Ven
characters.portrait_blurb: Ear-cuffed in cork. Fine lines around the eyes from listening intently for 40 years. Has never missed a vote.

fate.threat: Echo is admitted to Trinity. If Bali, Kali, and Fused ever relent, Echo joins the Trinity — and Trinity ends. See Trinity's fate for consequence. Echo itself becomes obsolete as a realm; its inhabitants would be absorbed.
fate.consequence: Echo realm ceases to be a separate realm. The Mirror Hall collapses to glass powder. The Half-Selves become full selves, returning to their original realms. The Reflection Sea evaporates. A new Tetrad realm replaces Trinity. Echo's purpose would be fulfilled — and Echo itself would be gone.
fate.prophecy_line: If you get what you wait for, you are no longer who waits. Echo knows. Echo waits anyway.
fate.destroyable_by: Trinity itself — by admitting Echo. This is a BENEVOLENT destruction of Echo as a realm, but a catastrophic one for Trinity. The prophetic ambiguity: which is the victim?

cultural_notes: Echo is the universe's exhibit of patience. Its culture is defined by waiting. Not despairing waiting — hopeful waiting. Every tribe believes in the possibility of admission without pretending it is likely. Visitors to Echo are often unsettled; staying long enough reveals Echo as one of the most genuinely kind realms in the cosmos. The Mirror-Tenders in particular are famously generous. Smurjan does not visit, out of respect. Mingus has never been reflected here — the mirrors will not show him. No one knows why.
